description: Establish foundational infrastructure and ML pipeline for NBA prediction model

stories.title: Python ML pipeline project setup
stories.description: Establish the Python ML pipeline scaffold: virtualenv, pinned dependencies, pytest + mypy config, and the directory layout the training jobs will live in.
stories.goal: Stand up a clean training scaffold that unblocks ingestion and model work
stories.notes: Foundational — every downstream backend story depends on this.

tasks.title: Bootstrap virtualenv and install ML pipeline dependencies
tasks.description: Create the project venv and pin the ML deps (xgboost, pandas, numpy, pyarrow).

tasks.title: Configure pytest and mypy with repo-wide defaults
tasks.description: Add pytest.ini and mypy.ini at the pipeline root so all jobs share a single config.

stories.title: Firebase Firestore configuration
stories.description: Stand up the Firestore backend used by the prediction service: project creation, SDK init, collection schemas, and security rules locked down against anonymous writes.
stories.goal: Provision the datastore that predictions are written to
acceptance_criteria: Firebase project created and credentials configured

tasks.title: Create Firebase project and load credentials via env
tasks.description: Provision the Firebase project, generate a service-account key, and wire it into the SDK via env vars.

tasks.title: Write Firestore security rules for predictions collection
tasks.description: Lock the predictions collection to authenticated reads only; no anonymous writes.
acceptance_criteria: Anonymous writes rejected in the rules test suite

stories.title: Which NBA stats features yield best XGBoost accuracy
stories.description: Investigate which NBA team-level statistics produce the highest XGBoost prediction accuracy, then publish a ranked feature list with expected impact to seed downstream model training.
stories.goal: Rank candidate features so the training pipeline starts from the strongest set
stories.notes: Spike output feeds the XGBoost training pipeline (issue 405).
acceptance_criteria: Feature importance analysis completed and documented
Top 10 features identified and ranked by importance
Feature set recommendations provided with pros/cons
Expected accuracy impact assessed for each feature

tasks.title: Run feature importance analysis on historical NBA data
tasks.description: Score every candidate feature against the historical NBA dataset and rank by XGBoost importance.

tasks.title: Draft feature recommendation memo with expected accuracy impact
tasks.description: Summarize the top features in a short memo noting which ones are worth bringing into training and the expected accuracy lift.

stories.title: Next.js 15 project setup
stories.description: Bootstrap the frontend with Next.js 15, TypeScript in strict mode, the App Router, and lint/format tooling so UI work can start on a clean baseline.
stories.goal: Give UI work a clean, strict-typed baseline

tasks.title: Initialize Next.js 15 project with App Router
tasks.description: Run create-next-app with the App Router preset and confirm a TypeScript hello-world renders.

tasks.title: Configure ESLint, Prettier, and tsconfig for strict mode
tasks.description: Land the shared lint/format config and turn on strict TS so future PRs catch type errors at commit time.

stories.title: NBA Stats API data ingestion
stories.description: Build the upstream data feed: a rate-limited NBA Stats API client that pulls team stats for the current season and persists raw responses to Parquet for downstream training.
stories.goal: Provide replayable raw training input from the NBA Stats API
stories.notes: Unblocked: depends on the ML scaffold (436) and its pytest config (509), both Done.
acceptance_criteria: NBA API client wrapper implemented with rate limiting
Team stats fetching working for current season

tasks.title: Build NBA Stats API client with rate limiting
tasks.description: Thin wrapper around the NBA Stats endpoints with a token bucket so we don't trip the upstream limiter.

tasks.title: Persist fetched team stats to Parquet files
tasks.description: Serialize each pull to a Parquet file keyed by season/date for replayable training input.

stories.title: Pre-market vs post-line-release prediction timing
stories.description: Compare prediction quality and data availability for pre-market vs post-line-release windows, then commit to a daily pipeline cadence with a recorded rationale in decisions.md.
stories.goal: Commit to a daily pipeline cadence with a recorded rationale
stories.notes: Blocked until the feature spike (447) lands so timing is judged on the real feature set.
acceptance_criteria: Trade-off analysis between pre-market and post-release completed
Cron schedule recommended for daily pipeline execution

tasks.title: Compare data availability windows for pre-market vs post-release
tasks.description: Enumerate what NBA data is fresh in each window and where the cutoffs land relative to a daily cron firing.

tasks.title: Record timing decision and propose cron schedule
tasks.description: Capture the chosen window in decisions.md and propose the daily cron schedule that follows from it.

stories.title: XGBoost model training pipeline
stories.description: Wire together the end-to-end training pipeline: feature engineering, cross-validated XGBoost training, and prediction writeback into Firestore.
stories.goal: Produce predictions end-to-end and write them to Firestore
stories.notes: Capstone story — blocked on the feature spike (by issue number) and the data feed (by title, the pre-mint form; sync converts it to the issue number).
acceptance_criteria: XGBoost model training with cross-validation working

tasks.title: Implement XGBoost training loop with cross-validation
tasks.description: K-fold the feature matrix and train XGBoost with early stopping; emit the trained model artifact.

tasks.title: Generate predictions and persist to Firestore
tasks.description: Score the test set with the trained model and write each prediction row to Firestore for the UI to consume.

stories.title: Investigate prediction caching layer
stories.description: Lean, ungroomed story authored straight into the backlog — exercises the lean shape (only title/description, defaults elsewhere) that must load and sync alongside fully-groomed items.
